title: Acadia's Remlifanserin Nears Phase III Completion for Alzheimer's Disease Psychosis
text: Acadia Pharmaceuticals' remlifanserin (ACP-204) is approaching Phase III completion for Alzheimer's disease psychosis treatment. Top-line Phase II data for remlifanserin in Alzheimer's psychosis are expected between August and October 2026. The compound is described as a successor to pimavanserin, the active ingredient in NUPLAZID, with potential for higher exposures without QT prolongation concerns. The Phase II study is placebo-controlled with three arms: placebo, 30 milligrams, and 60 milligrams. The primary endpoint is the SAPS-H+D score at week six, focused on hallucinations and delusions. The trial is powered at 80% for a 0.4 effect size. Acadia is also studying remlifanserin in Lewy body dementia psychosis, an indication with substantial unmet need. About 1 million patients have Lewy body dementia, and 50-75% experience psychosis. Successful Phase II data could unlock a multi-billion dollar opportunity.
